title: Release Skopeo 1.21.0
body:
- New support for creating "simple signing" signatures using Sequoia-PGP, dependent on a build tag that enables it
- New option `skopeo copy --force-compression-format`
- New option `--user-agent-prefix`
- TLS options on the command line of `skopeo sync` take precedence over options in YAML

This is intended to match dependencies of Podman 5.7 as closely as possible.

Cc: @siretart @mheon @TomSweeneyRedHat 

Note to self: Update `version.go` on `main` afterwards.

comments.body:
Wait, this _PR_ is wrong — it shouldn’t be merged against `main`; this needs to be a new branch, against the parent commit. (So, reviewers, please verify this is the correct parent, as well.)

After sufficient approval, I (or another maintainer) can manually create the branch + tag based on the incoming commit/branch.
(Marked as draft only to prevent merging with the head of the `main` branch.)

I assumed you branched of the old commit intentionally to then tag this exact commit as it is in this PR. Merging that into main would not change the tag content of this commit given its parent would still be the same. You just need to be sure to not tag the merge commit.

If we then later want a branch you could still branch of the point of the tag and push that as a branch.
https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/2759 filed to update the version number in `main`.
